mnemonics: PASSING THE INITIAL VALUES: Parameterized constructors pass initial values to objects, just like passing a note with instructions to a new worker.
keyPoints: Parameterized constructors allow object initialization with specific values at creation time.
They accept arguments to set the initial state of an object, making objects immediately usable.
They help avoid the need for separate setter calls after default construction, ensuring objects are always in a valid state.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to define a default constructor after defining a parameterized one, which can cause compilation errors if default construction is needed.
Providing incorrect data types or number of arguments when creating an object, leading to errors if no matching constructor is found.
